title: Abutilon leucopetalum
taxonomy: Abutilon leucopetalum is classified within the family Malvaceae. This classification places it within the order Malvales, a group of flowering plants that also includes hibiscus and okra. The genus Abutilon, to which Abutilon leucopetalum belongs, is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The species Abutilon leucopetalum is further classified within the family Malvaceae due to its characteristic flowers and growth habits. Abutilon leucopetalum's classification helps botanists understand its evolutionary relationships with other plant species within the Malvaceae family.

intro: Abutilon leucopetalum, also known as the white horse mallows, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Abutilon leucopetalum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Abutilon leucopetalum is commonly used to prepare topical creams, salves, and infusions, as well as teas, to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
